gene_region.label: DRD4 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical DRD4 gene interval used by NCBI Gene and ClinGen for the GRCh37/hg19 assembly.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to decide whether the commonly studied DRD4 promoter region was included in the search.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter polymorphism hotspot
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Approximate -809 to -521 interval highlighted in classic DRD4 promoter studies, including the rs747302 and rs1800955 regulatory hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: DRD4 encodes the dopamine D4 receptor, a dopamine-responsive GPCR that inhibits adenylyl cyclase and has been studied in attention, novelty-seeking, addiction, and schizophrenia-related phenotypes. On the hg19/GRCh37 assembly used by this project, DRD4 spans chr11:637,269-640,706 and covers 3,438 bp of the transcribed gene interval.
gene_context.clinical_context: The curated DRD4 records in this database are primarily research and pharmacogenomic context markers rather than high-penetrance diagnostic variants. When explicit ClinVar-style pathogenicity is not available, the app reports that status directly instead of implying a clinical-grade interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: Published DRD4 methylation studies have linked higher methylation to addiction risk, ADHD-related attentional symptoms, and male schizophrenia cohorts, but the direction and magnitude of those effects depend on tissue source and the exact CpG region that was measured. The bundled EPIC subset in this project mostly covers DRD4 gene-body and CpG-island probes plus one 3'UTR probe, so its beta values should be treated as regulatory context rather than a stand-alone diagnostic biomarker or a direct readout of the promoter assays used in many case-control papers.

evidence.label: NCBI Gene 1815: DRD4 genomic coordinates and summary
ClinGen gene page: DRD4 locus metadata on GRCh37
PMC5776619: DRD4 promoter hypermethylation and addiction risk
PMC3929639: male-specific DRD4 methylation association in schizophrenia
PubMed 26897359: DRD4 methylation and ADHD symptom dimensions

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs3758653 is best interpreted as an upstream regulatory DRD4 marker with methylation-QTL and behavioral-association evidence. Published studies link it to nearby DRD4 CpG methylation levels, executive-time differences, and time from first heroin exposure to addiction, but it is not treated as a monogenic pathogenic variant.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Research-level association; no pathogenic or likely pathogenic assertion is curated for this SNP in the local database.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: Frequently discussed as a promoter-adjacent regulatory SNP rather than a core coding change.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: This locus is especially relevant when DRD4 methylation is also being reviewed: rs3758653 has repeatedly been linked to DRD4 CpG methylation differences. In this workbench, methylation should still be read as supportive regulatory context because the bundled probe subset does not directly reproduce every promoter CpG from the published mQTL studies.

evidence.label: PubMed 34864042: rs3758653 mQTL and heroin addiction timing
PubMed 26037358: rs3758653 and executive-time differences in adults

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs747302 (-616C>G) is a commonly studied DRD4 promoter-region polymorphism with phenotype-association evidence rather than diagnostic pathogenicity. Large schizophrenia meta-analysis did not support a robust association for this site, and post-mortem brain tissue work did not show a statistically significant mRNA effect; separate neuroimaging work has reported genotype-related differences in pediatric primary nocturnal enuresis cohorts.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Research-level association; no pathogenic or likely pathogenic classification is seeded for this promoter SNP.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: One of the classic DRD4 promoter polymorphisms routinely reviewed in candidate-gene studies.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Regional methylation may still matter biologically around rs747302, but current evidence supports a regulatory-modifier role rather than a methylation-defined diagnostic effect. Use the paired methylation panel to contextualize local epigenetic state, not to over-call this promoter SNP.

evidence.label: PubMed 20468066: post-mortem mRNA study including rs747302

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1800955 (-521C>T) is a common DRD4 promoter polymorphism with research-level evidence for altered transcriptional regulation and small effect-size psychiatric associations. Early reporter assays found lower transcriptional efficiency for the T allele, while later post-mortem brain tissue work did not find a statistically significant genotype-expression relationship; meta-analysis suggests the CC genotype may modestly increase schizophrenia risk, but this site is not treated as a monogenic pathogenic variant.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Research-level association; no pathogenic or likely pathogenic ClinVar-style assertion is seeded for this promoter SNP.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: The best-known DRD4 promoter SNP in schizophrenia and emotion-processing candidate-gene studies.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Interpret rs1800955 alongside regional DRD4 methylation only as regulatory context. Differential methylation in the broader DRD4 locus has been reported in addiction, ADHD, and schizophrenia cohorts, but those effects are tissue-specific and do not convert rs1800955 into a diagnostic methylation biomarker.

evidence.label: PubMed 10329380: -521C>T transcriptional efficiency and schizophrenia association
PubMed 20468066: post-mortem brain mRNA study of rs1800955 and rs747302
PMC9321090 / PubMed 35574658: rs1800955 and emotion-processing phenotype

variant_records.common_name: a120-bp duplication in the 5' flanking region
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: A 120-bp duplication in the DRD4 5' flanking region has been reported as part of the classical promoter haplotype architecture and can alter transcription-factor binding context. It is biologically relevant in promoter studies, but the SNP-oriented VCF preview in this app does not directly genotype this duplication.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Research-level promoter structural variant; not directly assayable by the current SNP-focused search output.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: Often discussed alongside rs747302 and rs1800955 when reviewing DRD4 promoter haplotypes.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: If the duplication is of interest, the methylation panel can still provide regional epigenetic context, but it does not substitute for a structural variant call.

evidence.label: PubMed 10329380: DRD4 promoter polymorphisms and transcriptional efficiency
PMC3151559: review of DRD4 polymorphisms and function

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: The exon III 48-bp VNTR is the most studied DRD4 gene-body variant, especially the 7-repeat allele, which has been associated at the research level with ADHD, novelty-seeking, addiction-related traits, and altered receptor signaling. Its effects are context-dependent and it is not a stand-alone diagnostic pathogenic variant.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Functional research variant; no definitive monogenic pathogenic assertion is seeded for this repeat.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: The 7R allele is the canonical non-promoter DRD4 polymorphism discussed in behavioral-genetics literature.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: The methylation panel can contextualize the gene-body environment around DRD4, but it does not directly genotype the exon III repeat. Dedicated repeat-aware assays are still required if the VNTR itself is the main target.

evidence.label: PMC3151559: DRD4 exon III VNTR review and functional context
